title: cerastium arvense subsp. matrense
intro: Cerastium arvense subsp. matrense, commonly known as field chickweed or matrentia, is a type of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cerastium arvense subsp. matrense include reducing inflammation and pain, improving skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and aiding in wound healing. It can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile herb for various herbal preparations.
